english: happy; calamity, bkra-mi-śis-pa (bkra mi shis pa) tams-čád (thams cad) all manner of calamities. (The expression bkra-mi-śis (bkra mi shis) c dat. for: 'Woe to . . .' in our translation of the New Test. does not rest upon classic authority, but has been adopted as analogous to the above mentioned bkra-śis śog (bkra shis shog).) bkra-śis-čos-rdzoṅ (bkra shis chos rdzong) (*ta-śi-čọ-dzoṅ*) (*ta shi cho dzong*) 'Tassisudon' in Bhot., bkra-śis-lhun-po (bkra shis lhun po) (*ta-śi-hlụm-po*) (*ta shi hlum po*) 'Tashilunpo' in Tsaṅ.

1. brightness, lustre = mdaṅs (mdangs), also bkrag-mdaṅs (bkrag mdangs), e. g. of jewels. 2. beautiful appearance, colour, of the face or skin, also śa-bkrag (sha bkrag); śa bkrag-mdaṅs (sha bkrag mdangs) pure gloss of the skin Mil.; bkrag-čan (bkrag can) bright. bkrag- čór (bkrag chor) without gloss, dim.
1. to select, choose; mčóg-tu (mchog tu) bkrab (bkrab) exquisite, choice Lex. — 2 = ₖrâb-pa (khrab pa), skrâb-pa (skrab pa) W.
1. pf. of ₉rél-ba (grel ba) Cs., Tar. 124, 14. — 2. to appoint, lás-la (las la) to a business.

1. Sch.: pf. of a verb bkrá-ba (bkra ba), adorned, decorated (?) 2. Cs.: bkras (bkras) abbreviation for bkra-śis (bkra shis), bkras-btags (bkras btags) for bkra-śis ₖa-btags (bkra shis kha btags) = ₖa-btags (kha btags).
1. for dkri-ba (dkri ba) to wrap. — 2. for ₖríd-pa (khrid pa) to conduct. — 3. to try to acquire, to search for Dzl. ཉེ (nye) to lay up, = *śri-če* (*shri ce*) W.
for dkris-pa (dkris pa) v. dkri-ba (dkri ba).
1. Dzl. ༡༨, 5 (18, 5) 1. prob. an incorrect reading. 2. prov. instead of dkrŭg-pa (dkrug pa), v. ja bkrŭg (ja bkrug).
1. Cs. poor, indigent, hungry, sai ṕyogs (sa'i phyogs) bkr. (bkr.) a poor, barren country Stg. — 2. miserly, stingy C.

vb. to be hungry; adj. hungry; sbst. hunger B., C. where it is now used as the respectful term; bkres-skóm (bkres skom) hunger and thirst; bkres-skóm-pa-las čóg-par ˳byín-te (bkres skom pa las chog par byin te) leading after hunger and thirst to satiety; bkrés-rṅab-pa (bkres rngab pa) Sch.: to have a ravenous appetite.
1. a small furrow conveying water from a conduit (yúr-ba (yur ba)) to trees or plants; furrow between the beds of a garden; hence: 2. flower-bed.

(Ld. *skaṅ, ₖaṅ* (*skang, khang*)) 1. marrow, rkaṅ-már (rkang mar) id.; rkàṅ-bro-ma (rkang bro ma) tasting of marrow Sch.; rkáṅ-gi kóṅ-nas byáms-pa (rkang gi khong nas byams pa) love from the innermost heart Thgy. — 2. the upper part of the arm or thigh, or the large marrow-bones of them, dpúṅ-rkaṅ (dpung rkang), rlá-rkaṅ (rla rkang) Med. — 3. kernel of a nut etc. W. — 4. = rkaṅ-pa (rkang pa) no. 5, stalk; also quill of a feather. — 5. in compounds for rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa).

(resp. źabs (zhabs)) 1. foot. — 2. leg. — 3 (cf. lag-pa (lag pa)) hind-foot. — 4. lower part, lower end, e. g. of a letter, rkaṅ-pa-čan (rkang pa can) 'having a foot', so the nine letters are called that extend below the line (ཀ (ka), ཤ (sha) etc.) Glr. — 5. stem, stalk, esp. leaf-stalk, lo-rkáṅ (lo rkang). — 6 verse, metrical line; tsig-rkáṅ (tshig rkang), prop.: tsígs-su-bčad-pai rkáṅ-pa (tshigs su bcad pa'i rkang pa), id., tsig-rkáṅ mtar (tshig rkang mthar) nyis-śád ṫob (nyis shad thob) at the close of a verse a double shad is placed; hence: verse of the Bible Chr. Prot. — 7. base, foundation, rdzu-p̄rúl-gyi rkáṅ-pa bźi (rdzu phrul gyi rkang pa bzhi) Dzl. the four 'pillars' of performing miracles Trigl. fol. 17. Comp. *kaṅ-kyóg* (*kang kyog*) bandy-legged C. — rkaṅ-kri (rkang kri) a piece of cloth to wrap round the legs (Lat.: tibiale) Sch. — rkaṅ-kúm (rkang kum) Lex. w. e., prob. having a foot contracted by disease — rkaṅ-ₖór (rkang khor) bandy-legged Sch. rkaṅ-mgyógs-pa (rkang mgyogs pa) nimble-footed, rkaṅ-mgyógs-kyi rdzas lham-la byúgs-te (rkang mgyogs kyi rdzas lham la byugs te) oiling his boots with swiftfootedness, a miraculous ointment imparting this gift Glr., Tar. 67. — rkaṅ-gró (rkang gro) a vassal or subject paying his duty by serving as a messenger or
